DRE preserves the verified prefix of successful trajectories, edits the unnecessary continuation after an answer emerges, and prefers the edited version within the RL group to weaken the signal for redundant thinking.

title: Visual Verification Enables Inference-time Steering and Autonomous Policy Improvement
blurb: VERITAS is a generator-verifier framework that pairs a pre-trained generalist robot policy with a gradient-free visual verifier that scores candidate actions at inference time, improving performance without additional training. Fine-tuning policies on the verified self-generated rollouts matches the efficiency of expert demonstrations while requiring no human interventions.

title: All Smoke, No Alarm: Oracle Signals in Agent-Authored Test Code
blurb: This paper analyzes 86,156 test-file patches from 33,596 agent-authored GitHub PRs produced by Codex, Copilot, Devin, Cursor, and Claude Code, finding that 80.2% contain weak or no explicit assertions and verify nothing despite running code. A regression controlling for agent, PR size, and language shows strong oracle signals raise merge likelihood (OR = 1.28, p < 0.001), suggesting test-file counts overestimate verification strength and that practitioners should adopt oracle-aware quality checks.

title: NoiseTilt: Noise-Tilted Reverse Kernels for Diffusion Reward Alignment
blurb: NoiseTilt introduces the Noise-Tilted Reverse Kernel (NTRK), a reward-guided diffusion sampler that injects reward gradients through the noise term while leaving the pretrained reverse mean unchanged, requiring only a single sample per step. A whitening operator makes the gradient safe to inject as noise, and on aesthetic generation NTRK matches the best baseline's reward using 25 NFEs instead of 500, a 20x compute reduction.

title: Variable-Width Transformers
blurb: This paper proposes a "><"-shaped transformer that keeps early and late layers wide while narrowing the middle, using a parameter-free residual resizing mechanism to allocate capacity nonuniformly across depth. Across dense models from 200M to 2B parameters and a 3B MoE, it beats parameter-matched uniform baselines on language modeling loss while cutting FLOPs by 22% and KV cache memory and I/O by 15%.

title: ProvenanceGuard: Source-Aware Factuality Verification for MCP-Based LLM Agents
blurb: ProvenanceGuard is a source-aware verifier for MCP-based LLM agents that catches cross-source conflation, where a claim is correct but attributed to the wrong tool or evidence source. On 281 medical-domain MCP traces, it reached block F1 0.802 and source accuracy 0.858, detected all 50 injected attribution swaps, and repaired blocked answers via retrieval-augmented revision.

title: Learning from the Self-future: On-policy Self-distillation for dLLMs
blurb: d-OPSD is the first on-policy self-distillation framework built for diffusion LLMs, replacing the autoregressive prefix conditioning of existing methods with self-generated suffix conditioning and shifting supervision from the token level to the step level to match the denoising process. Across four reasoning benchmarks it outperforms RLVR and SFT baselines while using roughly 10% of the optimization steps required by RLVR.

title: Cordon: Semantic Transactions for Tool-Using LLM Agents
blurb: Cordon is a transactional runtime that stages and validates irreversible agent effects before commit, replacing per-call guardrails with a task-scoped execution boundary for commit, rollback, recovery, and audit across multi-step workflows. It tracks derived results, runs reversible mutations in shadow state, and stages outward actions in an effect outbox, exposing cross-step violations that existing defenses miss while preserving benign task completion at modest overhead.

title: NVIDIA Blackwell Tops MLPerf Training 6.0 with Industry-Leading Scale and Performance
blurb: NVIDIA swept MLPerf Training v6.0, posting the fastest time to train at scale and the highest per-accelerator performance on every benchmark, and was the only platform to submit results on all tests. Its GB300 NVL72 system, which links 72 Blackwell Ultra GPUs and 36 Grace CPUs over NVLink, set the bar on the round's new DeepSeek-V3 and GPT-OSS-20B pretraining workloads.

title: Riding High After I.P.O., SpaceX to Buy A.I. Start-Up for $60 Billion
blurb: SpaceX exercised its option to acquire AI coding startup Cursor in an all-stock deal valued at $60B, expanding Elon Musk's artificial intelligence ambitions. The acquisition follows SpaceX's recent IPO and gives Musk a foothold in AI-assisted software development alongside his existing xAI operations.

title: Wolfram Language and Mathematica version 15
blurb: Wolfram Research released Version 15 of Wolfram Language and Mathematica, adding extensive core functionality and new interfaces designed for AI systems alongside human users. The release extends the language's user base from humans to AIs, applying its coherent, formalized design so models can call its computational tools precisely and verify exactly what is being computed.

title: Stanford's DeLM cuts multi-agent task costs 50% — without a central orchestrator
blurb: Stanford researchers introduced DeLM, a decentralized language model framework that lets agents coordinate directly through a shared knowledge base instead of routing every update through a central orchestrator. By treating that knowledge base as a common communication substrate where agents build on one another's verified progress, DeLM removes the controller bottleneck and cuts multi-agent task costs by roughly 50%.

title: Volterra Generative Models
blurb: Volterra generative models are a continuous-time score-based framework that replaces the memoryless Brownian noise in diffusion with path-dependent perturbations drawn from fractional kernels, handled via finite-dimensional Markovian lifts. On MNIST and CIFAR-10, the authors show that persistent fractional perturbations with small lifts can improve score-based generation, with a Gaussian-bridge reconstruction sampler added to stabilize larger, stiffer lifts.

title: Compositional Skill Routing for LLM Agents: Decompose, Retrieve, and Compose
blurb: SkillWeaver is a decompose-retrieve-compose framework that breaks complex agent queries into sub-tasks, retrieves a matching skill for each via a FAISS bi-encoder, and assembles them into a dependency-aware DAG plan. Its Iterative Skill-Aware Decomposition loop lifts decomposition accuracy from 51.0% to 67.7% and cuts context window consumption by over 99% across a benchmark of 2,209 real MCP server skills.

title: Databricks says it solved the decades-old data pipeline problem that's been slowing AI agents
blurb: Databricks announced Lakehouse//RT and LTAP at its Data + AI Summit, two products aimed at removing the pipeline latency that slows AI agents acting on live data. Lakehouse//RT delivers millisecond query latency directly on governed Delta and Iceberg tables, while LTAP stores Postgres-native transactional data in those formats from the point of write, eliminating the ETL layer between operational and analytical systems.

title: Building the agentic data stack: A practical dbt guide for the AI era
blurb: This dbt guide lays out what a data project needs to support AI agents safely, built around model contracts, data tests, the dbt Semantic Layer, and the dbt Wizard agent. The authors note that only 7% of enterprises consider their data fully AI-ready, and that grounding LLMs in governed MetricFlow definitions raised natural-language query accuracy to about 83%, far above raw text-to-SQL.

title: Building AI Agents for AR Glasses and XR Devices with NVIDIA XR AI
blurb: NVIDIA released XR AI, an open source library now in public beta for building intelligent agents that run on AR glasses, AI glasses, and XR headsets. The agents see what users see, parse spoken or typed intent, and call enterprise tools within a single XR session, combining Cosmos for visual grounding, Nemotron for voice, MCP for enterprise connectivity, and the NeMo Agent Toolkit for orchestration.

title: Google Vertex AI SDK Flaw Let Attackers Hijack Model Uploads via Bucket Squatting
blurb: Google patched a flaw in the Vertex AI SDK for Python that let an attacker with only a victim's public project ID hijack model uploads and run code inside Google's serving infrastructure. Dubbed "Pickle in the Middle" by Palo Alto Networks Unit 42, the bug let attackers pre-create the SDK's predictable default staging bucket, swap in a malicious pickle-based model, and steal an OAuth token on load. Update to version 1.148.0 or later.

title: Seeing Is Not Screening: Multimodal Hidden Instruction Attacks on Agent Skill Scanners
blurb: This paper shows that AI agent skill scanners, which mostly analyze text descriptions, manifests, and source code, can be bypassed by malicious instructions hidden inside bundled images, an attack the authors call SkillCamo that rewrites documentation to make agents interpret the visual payload at execution time. To counter it, they propose ExecScan, an execution-grounded multimodal module that jointly analyzes docs, code, resources, and images to reconstruct behavior chains and flag risks like exfiltration, persistence, and privilege escalation.

title: Uncertainty Quantification for Flow-Based Vision-Language-Action Models
blurb: This paper introduces a method for quantifying epistemic uncertainty in flow-based vision-language-action models using velocity-field disagreement (VFD) across a small ensemble, enabling failure detection during deployment. The authors also propose SAVE, an uncertainty-guided active fine-tuning framework that adapts VLAs to new tasks with at least 22% fewer expert demonstrations on the LIBERO benchmark.

title: Your Privacy My Cloak: Backdoor Attacks on Differentially Private Federated Learning
blurb: This paper challenges the assumption that differential privacy makes federated learning more robust against backdoor attacks, showing instead that DP compliance masks the statistical signals defenses rely on to filter malicious updates. The authors introduce RING, a backdoor-agnostic perturbation layer in which compromised clients collaboratively craft adversarial updates that reconstruct a strong backdoor signal during aggregation while evading anomaly detection.

title: PARSE: Provenance-Aware Retrieval Sanitization for Professional Domain LLM Agents
blurb: PARSE is a provenance-aware sanitization pipeline that defends LLM agents against prompt injection in real enterprise documents by classifying each sentence's injection likelihood, extracting structured facts before rewriting, and verifying fact preservation through a consistency-checking loop. Tested on 122 tasks across financial, legal, medical, scientific, and DevOps domains, it cut attack success rate from 25.4% to 15.6% while holding utility at 86.9%, whereas paraphrasing showed no significant reduction on real documents.

title: Meta launches AI Mode in Facebook search to answer questions
blurb: Meta launched AI Mode in Facebook Search, which uses Meta AI to deliver generated answers drawn from public content across Facebook Groups, Reels, and other Meta apps instead of a standard list of results. Meta said responses are grounded in real experiences and recommendations but did not explain how AI Mode selects which posts, Groups, or Reels appear.

title: Openrouter Fusion API
blurb: OpenRouter released Fusion, an API that routes a prompt to a panel of expert models running in parallel with web search enabled, then uses a judge model to synthesize their outputs into a structured analysis covering consensus, contradictions, and blind spots. Requests are priced as the sum of every panel member plus the judge call, and users can swap between Quality and Budget presets or override the panel and judge models entirely.

title: ConSA: Controllable Sparsity in Hybrid Attention via Learnable Allocation
blurb: ConSA is a framework that learns how to allocate full attention and sliding-window attention across a hybrid LLM under a user-specified sparsity target, using L0 regularization and an augmented Lagrangian constraint at the layer or KV-head level. Tested on 0.6B and 1.7B models, its learned allocations beat rule-based baselines, placing sliding-window attention in bottom layers and concentrating full attention in contiguous middle-layer blocks.

title: AMD's Mext buy shows how AI could solve the RAM shortage it created
blurb: AMD acquired Mext, a 2023 startup whose machine learning platform offloads underused data from RAM to cheaper flash storage and restores it before applications need it, for an undisclosed sum. Mext claims it can expand a system's effective memory by 2 to 4x, and AMD plans to apply the approach to offload infrequently used mixture-of-experts components and run larger models on fewer resources.

title: Fixed-Point Reasoners: Stable and Adaptive Deep Looped Transformers
blurb: This paper introduces FPRM, a Transformer-based looped architecture that uses fixed-point convergence as an end-to-end halting mechanism, letting the model adapt its compute to each task's difficulty. The authors combine pre-norm layers and residual scaling to fix the depth-induced signal propagation problem in looped models, and report strong results on Sudoku, Maze, state-tracking, and ARC-AGI.

title: [AINews] GLM-5.2: the top Frontend Coding model in the world, IndexShare for Speculative Decoding
blurb: Z.ai released GLM-5.2, an MIT-licensed open-weight model with a 1M-token context window and two reasoning-effort modes, at the same API pricing as GLM-5.1. Third-party leaderboards place the 744B-parameter model just behind Opus 4.8 as the best coding model overall and ahead of every Opus version on frontend coding, with day-zero support across Transformers, vLLM, and SGLang.

title: How to Optimize Transformer-Based Models for Low-Precision Training
blurb: This NVIDIA post walks through how to determine the optimal numerical precision for transformer training before committing GPU hours, by converting a model config and batch size into the exact M×K×N GEMM shapes the model executes and benchmarking them across BF16, FP8, and NVFP4. Using NVIDIA Transformer Engine and a single microbenchmark command, the workflow profiles those matrix multiplications and estimates where lower precision actually yields speedups, demonstrated on the 5B-parameter CodonFM RNA language model.

title: AI and brain-computer interface allow speechless ALS patient to work a full-time job
blurb: A UC Davis research team paired a brain-computer interface with machine learning to let a speechless ALS patient decode intended speech from neural activity, enabling him to hold a full-time job. The hardware itself isn't new, but the team's ML-powered decoding method translates the patient's attempted speech into text and synthesized voice fast enough to support real-time work and conversation.

title: “성능 향상 vs 데이터 우려”…스페이스X의 커서 인수에 엇갈린 평가
blurb: SpaceX officially announced it will acquire AI coding startup Cursor for $60B in an all-stock deal, with a $10B breakup fee if SpaceX walks away. Analysts are split, with some arguing that access to xAI's GPU capacity will unblock Cursor's growth toward $2B in annual revenue, while others flag data, governance, and model-choice concerns for the enterprise customers behind 64% of the Fortune 500.

title: Kolmogorov Regression for Robust Diffusion Policies
blurb: This paper introduces Kolmogorov regression, which lifts finite-dimensional diffusion policies into a Cameron-Martin space and replaces stochastic score matching with a deterministic boundary-value PDE, adding a Kolmogorov residual as a reward-free failure detector at inference. On the PushT manipulation benchmark, the Cameron-Martin loss raised maximum episode reward to 0.95 from 0.78 and cut inter-step drift by 67.6%, while a manufacturing line task beat LSTM baselines by 28.4% RMSE.

title: WEQA: Wearable hEalth Question Answering with Query-Adaptive Agentic Reasoning
blurb: WEQA is a query-adaptive agent framework that pairs an LLM controller with specialized wearable analysis and prediction tools to answer questions about continuous sensor data. Routing each query to the right combination of models and auditing responses against external knowledge, it scored 24% higher than LLM and agentic baselines across four wearable datasets and earned usefulness gains in a blinded study with 12 medical experts and 8 users.

title: From pixels to planning: Earth AI for nature restoration
blurb: Google Research released a vectorized version of its Farmscapes dataset, converting high-resolution raster maps into an actionable inventory of hedgerows, stone walls, and copses across the UK. Built with a deep learning framework that detects fine-scale woody features invisible to standard satellite imagery, the vector data lets landowners and conservationists measure and expand carbon-storing habitat without displacing cropland.

title: Anthropic, Trump Officials Seek Deal on Restoring Powerful Model Access
blurb: Anthropic and Trump administration officials are negotiating to restore access to the company's Fable and Mythos models after Anthropic pulled them. In its first public comments since the removal, Anthropic said both parties are working quickly to resolve the matter, though neither side detailed what prompted the loss of access or the terms under discussion.

title: Why Weibo’s tiny VibeThinker-3B has the AI world arguing over benchmarks again
blurb: Weibo researchers released VibeThinker-3B, a 3B-parameter reasoning model that scored 94.3 on AIME 2026, matching the 671B-parameter DeepSeek V3.2 and edging out Gemini 3 Pro's 91.7. The results have split the research community, with a test-time scaling method called Claim-Level Reliability Assessment pushing the score to 97.1 and reviving doubts over whether AI benchmarks remain meaningful.

title: Edge Flow: A Tractable and Predictive Continuous-Time Model for Gradient Descent at the Edge of Stability
blurb: Edge Flow is a continuous-time model of gradient descent at the edge of stability, where the largest Hessian eigenvalue hovers near the 2/η threshold that classical gradient flow analysis cannot handle. It uses three coupled ODEs that separate a center, an oscillation direction, and an oscillation magnitude, requiring only two gradient evaluations and one Hessian-vector product per step while tracking sharpness oscillation at least as faithfully as prior models.

title: Security and Privacy Prompts in the Wild: What Users Ask LLMs and How LLMs Respond
blurb: This study analyzes WildChat's 3.2M user-LLM conversations to identify 14,727 real security and privacy prompts, the first work to examine the S&P questions people actually ask rather than expert-authored FAQs. Testing 270 advice-seeking prompts 10 times each, the authors found GPT 5.5 gave "good enough" answers on 98% of prompts versus 47% for Llama 4, though commercial models sometimes returned contradictory advice across runs.

title: LegalHalluLens: Typed Hallucination Auditing and Calibrated Multi-Agent Debate for Trustworthy Legal AI
blurb: LegalHalluLens is an auditing framework that profiles legal AI hallucinations across four claim categories (numeric, temporal, obligation/entitlement, and factual) and introduces a Risk Direction Index to capture whether a model omits or invents. Across 510 contracts, the authors found 38-40 pp within-model gaps that aggregate metrics hide, and their typed debate pipeline cut fabricated detections by 45% while matching commercial APIs with a 4B-parameter backbone.

title: GameCraft-Bench: Can Agents Build Playable Games End-to-End in a Real Game Engine?
blurb: GameCraft-Bench is a benchmark of 140 Godot tasks across 15 game families that tests whether coding agents can turn natural-language specs into complete, playable games inside a real engine, scored through replayed gameplay and rubric-guided multimodal judging. The strongest frontier agent reached only 41.46% and most scored below 40%, often implementing recognizable mechanics but failing to deliver sufficient content, functional visual feedback, and coherent presentation.

title: EvolveNav: Proactive Preflection and Self-Evolving Memory for Zero-Shot Object Goal Navigation
blurb: EvolveNav is a zero-shot object-goal navigation framework that improves at test time by building an agentic rule memory from past trajectories and retrieving rules with an upper-confidence-bound strategy that balances semantic relevance and historical success. A memory-guided preflection module forecasts action outcomes before the agent moves, and the method beats existing zero-shot baselines with a 10.1% gain in success rate and fewer unnecessary steps.

title: The Stanford EDGAR Filings Dataset: Reconstructing U.S. Corporate and Financial Disclosures into Layout-Faithful and Token-Efficient Pretraining Data
blurb: Researchers introduced the Stanford EDGAR Filings Dataset (SEFD), an open reconstruction of SEC filings into layout-faithful MultiMarkdown to provide clean long-context pretraining data as high-quality web corpora run dry. The release includes SEFD-v1, a 152B-token snapshot with under 0.1% overlap with Common Crawl, plus two benchmarks: EDGAR-Forecast for filing-grounded numerical forecasting and EDGAR-OCR for transcribing complex financial tables.

title: RubricsTree: Scalable and Evolving Open-Ended Evaluation of Personal Health Agents across Health Memory and Medical Skills
blurb: RubricsTree is an evaluation framework for personal health agents built on an expert-aligned hierarchical taxonomy of over 100 atomic, clinically-verifiable Boolean rubrics, curated from 4,000 real user queries with a physician-led panel. A context-aware router activates only the relevant rubric subset per query, and using its outputs as instructions, feedback, or training rewards yielded up to roughly 66% relative gains on HealthBench for Gemini, GPT, and Qwen models.

title: Trust the Right Teacher: Quality-Aware Self-Distillation for GUI Grounding
blurb: This paper proposes quality-aware self-distillation for training vision-language models on GUI grounding, where the model must locate small UI elements and predict precise screen coordinates. The method adds soft correctness-aware gating, which down-weights teacher signals once a generated prefix has drifted from the target box, plus teacher-probability scaling to calibrate the remaining supervision, improving results across six grounding benchmarks.

title: Rethinking Dataset Distillation for Classification: Do Distilled Sets Outperform Coresets?
blurb: This paper benchmarks seven state-of-the-art dataset distillation methods against coreset selection on ImageNet-1K, ImageNet100, and ImageNette using standardized protocols, challenging the common claim that distilled synthetic sets beat real-sample subsets. The authors find that top distillation methods are comparable to or worse than coresets on large-scale data while costing far more to build, and that coresets achieve better coverage of the original distribution.

title: Unintended Effects of Geographic Conditioning in Large Language Models
blurb: This paper evaluates "location leakage," where LLMs generate region-specific references despite geographically neutral prompts when user location metadata is present in hidden context. Injecting location raised leakage by up to 793 times above baseline (0.04% to 31.7% for Llama 3.1-8B), and even replacing the location with an "Unknown" placeholder elevated it up to 72 times, showing the user profile frame itself acts as a conditioning signal.

title: US Cracks Down on Anthropic AI Models Amid Abuse Concerns
blurb: Anthropic abruptly suspended all access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 after receiving a US export control directive barring foreign nationals from using the models. The order followed abuse concerns flagged by regulators, cutting off international users with no advance notice and raising questions about how export rules will apply to frontier AI access going forward.

title: Differential Privacy of Gaussian Process Posterior Sampling
blurb: This paper studies the privacy of releasing Gaussian process posterior sample paths and shows that the sampling's intrinsic randomness yields differential-privacy guarantees without external noise, deriving explicit Rényi-DP bounds that depend sharply on ridge regularization. Membership-inference attacks confirm the predicted leakage behavior, and the authors show that adding calibrated GP noise provides an additional knob when stronger privacy is required.

title: Anthropic’s new privacy policy offers US consumers a way around the Fable ban
blurb: The US government ordered Anthropic to suspend access to its new Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models for all foreign nationals on national security grounds, but the company says it cannot distinguish foreign users from US citizens, forcing a blanket shutdown. An update to its consumer privacy policy, taking effect July 8, gives Anthropic a way to identify US customers and restore their access.

title: First Proof Second Batch
blurb: This paper tests several AI systems on ten research-level mathematics problems that arose naturally in the work of contributing mathematicians across a broad range of fields. The authors document the problems, their testing methodology, and the results, and provide supplementary links to the human solutions, the AI-generated solutions, and referee reports evaluating each AI attempt. The setup measures whether current models can solve genuine research problems rather than benchmark-style exercises.

title: ShellGames: Speculative LLM-Driven SSH Deception
blurb: ShellGames is an LLM-driven SSH shell simulator for cyber deception that combines chain-of-thought prompting, memory-based state tracking, speculative command execution, sandbox routing for complex commands, and subversion detection. It reaches 0.898 command accuracy and 0.918 sequence-level consistency, and a 20-person user study found its realism comparable to a real shell and ahead of traditional honeypots on perceived command coverage.

title: Qualcomm launches Snapdragon Reality Elite and a white-label toolkit for AI glasses, betting the next platform is not a phone
blurb: Qualcomm announced Snapdragon Reality Elite, a mixed reality chip platform with 160% higher NPU performance than the prior XR2+ Gen 2, alongside START, a white-label toolkit that hands eyewear makers a near-complete smart glasses design to brand and ship. CEO Cristiano Amon told CNBC the company is working on more than 40 AI wearable form factors, from camera-equipped earbuds to pins and watches.

title: OTRO: Oblivious Tokenization Path with Square-Root ORAM
blurb: OTRO is an oblivious tokenization path that hides the memory access patterns of CPU-side LLM tokenizers, which recent work showed can leak entire user prompts on production Intel TDX. Using a pool of replicated square-root ORAM instances with epoch-based rotation and KV-cache-aware tokenization, it caps time-to-first-token overhead at 4.5% while adding under 0.5 GB of memory, far cheaper than PathORAM's roughly 13x slowdown.

title: An Empirical Analysis of AI Slop in Music Streaming
blurb: A new paper studies the economics of AI-generated "slop" music on streaming platforms and concludes it is poised to become a self-sustaining shadow industry. Analyzing Spotify, the authors found 93% of AI tracks get few or no plays, with artists "spray and pray" flooding genres for a rare hit. They published their own AI songs through 11 indie distributors, finding inconsistent and largely unenforced policies, and showed that current AI-music detectors lack accuracy and robustness.

title: Can Europe train a frontier AI model on the compute it owns?
blurb: A sourced model argues Europe could field a frontier-class AI model by federating the public compute it already owns, rather than waiting on planned gigawatt datacenters. The EuroHPC supercomputers and national AI Factories total tens of exaflops, while a 1 GW campus waits a mean of 7.6 years for grid power. Using low-communication DiLoCo-style training, the existing fleet could deliver a model around 2028, against roughly 2033 for a new campus.

title: From Reasoning Traces to Reusable Modules: Understanding Compositional Generalization in Language Model Reasoning
blurb: This paper argues that the combined success of supervised fine-tuning and reinforcement learning in LLM reasoning stems from compositional generalization, formalized through a hierarchical latent selection model where reasoning traces decompose into reusable atomic modules. The authors show SFT and RL play complementary roles, with SFT supplying compositional traces and RL extracting and recombining atomic modules to solve new configurations.

title: Qualcomm mulls taking over Jim Keller's Tenstorrent, report claims — deal for AI chipmaker would value the company at between $8 billion and $10 billion
blurb: Qualcomm is evaluating an acquisition of Jim Keller's Tenstorrent that could value the AI chipmaker at between $8B and $10B, according to The Information. The report says Qualcomm is mainly after Tenstorrent's RISC-V-based AI accelerators and data center CPU IP, though the deal would leave it juggling two AI accelerator lines and three data center CPU designs.

title: Tensor-based second-order causal discovery
blurb: This paper introduces Tensor-based Second-order Causal Discovery (TSCD), an algorithm that takes a tensor built from the covariance matrices of observational and interventional data and outputs a DAG plus its edge functions under a linear structural equation model, requiring only uncorrelated noise. The authors show identifiable causal order and parameters from a number of interventions logarithmic in the variable count, with experiments scaling to hundreds of variables.

title: Bifrost: Hybrid TEE-FHE Inference for Privacy-Preserving Transformer and LLM Serving
blurb: Bifrost is a hybrid TEE-FHE serving architecture that provisions secrets only to an attested CPU TEE, using FHE to delegate projection and feed-forward linear layers to an untrusted accelerator while non-linear and KV-state operators run inside the TEE. Bifrost+ adds a prefill/decode split that cuts TTFT by 14.6-53.4x on GPT-2 and Qwen3 in direct CKKS deployments.

title: Enabling Governed Vibe Coding for Enterprise Apps on Databricks
blurb: Databricks introduced three capabilities for Databricks Apps that bring governed "vibe coding" to enterprise app development: App Spaces, Genie App Builder, and Serverless Micro Apps. App Spaces lets admins define data access, API scopes, and security policies once at the space level so every app inherits them automatically, while Genie App Builder turns a plain-language description or screenshot into a working internal app.

title: VoidPadding: Let [VOID] Handle Padding in Masked Diffusion Language Models so that [EOS] Can Focus on Semantic Termination
blurb: VoidPadding is a technique for masked diffusion language models that introduces a dedicated [VOID] token for padding, freeing [EOS] to serve solely as a semantic terminator and fixing the [EOS] overflow that plagues large-block decoding. On Dream-7B-Instruct, it raises the block-size-averaged mean across math and code benchmarks by 17.84 points over the base model while cutting decoding NFE by 55.7% on average.

title: What the Anthropic shutdown means for Europe
blurb: The US government ordered Anthropic to suspend access to its most advanced models for foreign nationals, prompting the company to cut off all customers, including those in the US, to ensure compliance. The episode marks the first activation of a US digital "kill switch" capable of severing European organizations from technologies they rely on for critical services, and has intensified European calls for sovereign AI infrastructure.

title: Sources: a new AI lab founded by Junyang Lin, former lead of Alibaba's Qwen models, has raised several hundred million dollars at a ~$2B post-money valuation (The Information)
blurb: Junyang Lin, the former lead of Alibaba's Qwen models, has founded a new AI lab that raised several hundred million dollars at a roughly $2B post-money valuation. The financing values the months-old startup near the level of established labs, underscoring continued investor appetite for teams with frontier-model pedigrees.

title: Leaked financial docs show OpenAI is losing billions of dollars a year
blurb: Leaked audited financials show OpenAI's revenue grew from $3.7B in 2024 to $13.07B in 2025, with monthly revenue nearing $2B by year-end. Its loss from operations widened from $8.78B to $20.92B over the same period, driven by $19.18B in R&D, as the company pursues an expected IPO and targets profitability by 2030.

title: D.O.J. Seeks to Halt Pollution Lawsuit Against Elon Musk’s Data Center
blurb: The Justice Department asked a federal court to halt a citizen-led pollution lawsuit against xAI's data center in Mississippi, which powers Elon Musk's Grok chatbot. The department cited national security, arguing xAI played a crucial role in the Iran war, and claimed authority to stop environmental suits brought by citizens.

title: Beyond Native Success: Auditing Deployment-Interface Exposure of CLIP Backdoors
blurb: This paper introduces DIFE, a framework that audits backdoored CLIP checkpoints across deployment interfaces like feature extraction, retrieval, reranking, and selection, finding that native attack success does not certify checkpoint-level risk and that exposure tracks specific reusable components. The authors also present BadTextTower, a text-side attack that produces strong text-conditioned retrieval and reranking exposure while leaving visual-only reuse nearly clean.

title: Build Your Own Transaction Foundation Model for Financial Intelligence
blurb: NVIDIA released a developer example for building a transaction foundation model end-to-end, pretraining a transformer decoder on unlabeled transaction sequences to produce general-purpose representations of financial behavior. The five-step workflow uses GPU-accelerated processing with cuDF and NeMo AutoModel, and reproduces a near-50% lift in Average Precision over a strong XGBoost baseline on the IBM TabFormer fraud dataset.

title: What are git worktrees, and why should I use them?
blurb: This GitHub blog post explains git worktrees, which let developers check out multiple branches into separate sibling folders simultaneously instead of stashing changes and switching branches in a single working directory. Worktrees have become the default workflow for parallel AI coding sessions and tools like the GitHub Copilot app, though each worktree requires its own copy of project dependencies.

title: Anthropic "pauses" token-based billing for its Claude Agent SDK
blurb: Anthropic paused a billing change for its Claude Agent SDK that would have charged outside SDK and programmatic usage at standard API rates instead of drawing from subscribers' existing plan limits. The reversal, announced just as the June 15 change was set to take effect, preserves the generous weekly caps that let Claude power users extract far more value from a subscription than equivalent API spend.

title: China’s DeepSeek reportedly raises $7.4B in funding at $50B+ valuation
blurb: DeepSeek reportedly raised $7.4B at a valuation above $50B, making it China's most valuable AI startup, with founder Liang Wenfeng contributing $3B and Tencent considering a $1.48B stake. The raise follows DeepSeek-V4-Pro, a 1.6T-parameter mixture-of-experts model whose cost efficiency has drawn interest from Microsoft as a lower-cost option for its Cowork Copilot app.

title: Sweeping Android 17 update brings new AI capabilities and features to Pixel smartphones
blurb: Google released Android 17, rolling out first to Pixel devices with new AI models, call-management tools, and multitasking features. The update adds support for Gemini Omni, Lyria 3, and AudioLM for speech recognition, multimodal interactions, and music generation, alongside a "Bubble Bar" interface for switching between recently used apps.

title: ReproRepo: Scaling Reproducibility Audits with GitHub Repository Issues
blurb: ReproRepo is a scalable framework for reproducibility evaluation that uses human-raised GitHub issues as naturally occurring supervision on real reproduction blockers, instantiated across 1,149 recent machine learning papers. The best configuration, Codex with GPT-5.5, surfaced at least one semantically related human-reported blocker for roughly 90% of papers without executing code, though agents still struggled with exact localization.

title: DRFLOW: A Deep Research Benchmark for Personalized Workflow Prediction
blurb: DRFLOW is a benchmark for evaluating how well deep research agents predict personalized, multi-step workflows from scattered enterprise sources, rather than generating the reports and summaries that prior work targets. It contains 100 tasks across five domains with 1,246 reference workflow steps grounded in over 3,900 sources, and its reference agent DRFA improves average F1 by up to 10.02% while leaving substantial headroom across the seven diagnostic metrics.

title: Towards Understanding and Measuring COGNITIVE ATROPHY in LLM Behaviour
blurb: This paper formalizes "cognitive atrophy," a process-level measure of whether LLMs in mental-health conversations help users keep reflecting and deciding for themselves rather than fostering dependence. The authors introduce Cognitive Atrophy Bench, built from 1,576 human counseling conversations and 5,324 clinician judgments, and find that all five tested models show moderate-to-high atrophy-aligned behavior, defaulting to directive advice and validation that can reinforce dependence.

title: AMD takes over MEXT for memory tiering tech that enables flash to appear as DRAM to applications — tech to 'address growing memory constraints' in the data center
blurb: AMD acquired MEXT, whose AI-based memory tiering makes NAND flash appear as DRAM to applications, cutting data-center DRAM costs.

title: IsabeLLM: Automated Theorem Proving Applied to Formally Verifying Consensus
blurb: This paper upgrades IsabeLLM, an automated theorem proving tool for the Isabelle proof assistant, with retrieval-augmented generation, error tracing, and counterexample generation to give the LLM better context. The authors test the new version against the original by formally verifying Bitcoin's Proof of Work consensus protocol, also adding compatibility with current Isabelle and Sledgehammer releases for efficiency.

title: S4oP: Operator-level Pruning of Structured State Space Models for Resource-Constrained Devices
blurb: This paper introduces S4oP, an operator-level pruning method for S4 and S4D structured state space models that incrementally interleaves structured masking with fine-tuning while monitoring accuracy and latency. Across multiple benchmarks, pruning up to 70% of operators preserved original performance in most cases while substantially cutting inference latency, which the authors present as the first systematic study of structured operator pruning for SSMs.

title: ConTex: Reformulating Counterfactual Generation For Time Series Forecasting
blurb: ConTex reformulates counterfactual generation for time series forecasting as a single globally consistent intervention strategy, replacing per-instance optimization with one shared function that produces sparse, interpretable interventions in a single forward pass. Across multiple forecasting architectures and benchmarks, it reaches state-of-the-art validity while cutting computational cost 12-36x and supporting real-time inference at roughly 0.007 seconds.

title: Sources: the US has held off adding DeepSeek, CXMT, and 100+ other flagged companies to its Entity List, last updated in October, the longest gap in 10+ years (Karen Freifeld/Reuters)
blurb: The US has not added DeepSeek, memory maker CXMT, and more than 100 other flagged companies to its trade restriction Entity List since the last update in October, sources said. The roughly eight-month pause is the longest gap in over a decade, signaling reluctance to escalate export curbs amid ongoing trade negotiations with China.

title: DeepSeek Becomes China’s Most Valuable AI Startup After $7.4 Billion Fundraise
blurb: DeepSeek raised more than $7.4 billion in its first external funding round, making it China's most valuable AI startup. The round backs the company's compute-heavy model development, an expense that has been difficult to sustain after DeepSeek built a global reputation on low-cost training and open-weight releases.

title: ReAge3D: Re-Aging 3D Faces with View Consistency
blurb: ReAge3D is a framework for realistic, identity-preserving 3D face re-aging that avoids the over-smoothing of fine age details common to existing 3D editing methods. It pairs DiffReaging, a 2D diffusion model trained on synthetic image pairs, with a center-out propagation strategy that warps from a re-aged frontal view and uses Masked-DiffReaging to keep all views consistent before optimizing the 3D representation.

title: PracRepair: LLM-Empowered Automated Program Repair Inspired by Human-Like Debugging Practices
blurb: PracRepair is an LLM-based automated program repair framework that mimics human debugging by building on-demand static-dynamic context, running question-driven failure diagnosis to form repair hypotheses, and iteratively refining patches using validation diagnostics and trace-level behavioral changes. On Defects4J, it fixes 162 bugs on V1.2 and 171 on V2.0 under GPT-4o, outperforming state-of-the-art baselines and generalizing to real-world bugs across multiple models.

title: The Measurement Gap in the Automation of EU Law: Benchmarking Doctrinal Legal Reasoning under the EU AI Act
blurb: LLMs now produce legal text of at least median quality, but this paper argues no existing benchmark tests whether they perform doctrinal legal reasoning, the interpretive core of legal work, rather than the paralegal tasks most legal-AI evaluations measure. The authors frame this as a legal problem, not just a methodological one: the EU AI Act makes "appropriate accuracy" a binding requirement for high-risk judicial AI, yet that requirement stays empty without the doctrinal-reasoning benchmark the field lacks.

title: ARES: A Platform for Adaptive Role-Based Evaluation of Social Engineering Risks in Human--AI Games
blurb: ARES is an open platform and pilot dataset for auditing adaptive social engineering risks in LLM-mediated decision-making through controlled social games spanning human-human, human-AI, and AI-AI settings. The pilot release captures 340 GB of multimodal data from 15 participants playing an adapted Prisoner's Dilemma and Ultimatum Game against a role-conditioned GPT-5.4 agent, spanning interaction logs, video, gaze, and smartwatch signals.

title: Sources: Jeff Bezos is investing in Cambridge, UK-based CuspAI, which applies generative AI to material sciences, as part of a $400M round at a $2.6B valuation (Tim Bradshaw/Financial Times)
blurb: Jeff Bezos is investing in CuspAI, a Cambridge, UK-based startup that applies generative AI to materials science, as part of a $400M round at a $2.6B valuation. The company uses AI to discover new materials, targeting applications such as carbon capture and energy storage.

title: Learning Red Agent Policy from Observations for Neurosymbolic Autonomous Cyber Agents
blurb: This paper introduces an imitation learning technique that lets a cyber-defense agent infer an unseen attacker's actions from network observations and its own defensive moves. Built for partially observable RL environments with discrete states and actions, the method integrates with a neurosymbolic defender using behavior trees and achieves high prediction accuracy across diverse simulated red-agent policies.

title: 데이터브릭스, 기업 업무 자동화 지원 AI 플랫폼 ‘지니 원’ 공개
blurb: Databricks released Genie One, an enterprise AI agent platform that connects company data, documents, applications, and people through a "Genie Ontology" to automate work and ground responses in governed data rather than inference. The launch includes Genie Agents for sharing reusable agents and Genie Code, available now across web, iOS, and Android, with usage-based pricing and up to $10 per user in monthly free credits.

title: Learning Fair Pareto-Optimal Policies in Multi-Objective Reinforcement Learning
blurb: This paper formalizes fair optimization in multi-policy multi-objective reinforcement learning, where the goal is to learn a set of Pareto-optimal policies that stay equitable across all possible user preferences. The authors show that policies under concave welfare functions like the generalized Gini remain in the convex coverage set, and propose three algorithms extending multi-objective Q-Learning to non-stationary and stochastic policies.

title: Multi-Source Cybersecurity Logs: An ATT&CK-Labeled Dataset and SLM Evaluation
blurb: This paper introduces a multi-source cybersecurity log dataset of 870 sessions and roughly 2.3 million events, capturing system, network, and browser activity on Windows endpoints with per-entry MITRE ATT&CK technique labels across 12 tactics and 53 techniques. Fine-tuning three small language models with LoRA lifted chunk classification accuracy from about 8% to 90-97%, though exact-match technique identification topped out at 42%.

title: Non-negative Elastic Net Decoding for Information Retrieval
blurb: This paper reframes information retrieval as a joint decoding problem, proposing Non-Negative elastic Net (NNN) decoding, which selects documents whose embeddings jointly reconstruct the query embedding as a sparse non-negative linear combination to reduce redundancy. The authors prove NNN strictly dominates standard dense retrieval and show consistent benchmark gains on frozen embeddings, with an end-to-end training procedure surpassing inner-product scoring across all metrics.

title: AWS Weekly Roundup: AWS FinOps Agent in preview, Gemma 4 on Bedrock, Kiro Pro Max, and more (June 15, 2026)
blurb: AWS released its FinOps Agent in preview and added Google's Gemma 4 to Bedrock, alongside the Kiro Pro Max tier, at the NYC Summit.

title: An AI Security Agent for Banking: Multi-Vector Fraud and AML Detection Across Retail and Corporate Accounts
blurb: This paper presents an AI security agent for retail and corporate banking that runs parallel transaction and session streams, each fusing an LSTM behavioral model, a velocity monitor, and a graph module for money-laundering patterns. On a synthetic log of 237,669 transactions and 113,508 sessions across 13 threat categories, it reached F1 scores of 0.787 and 0.867, beating rule-based and LSTM-only baselines.

title: A Framework for Evaluating Agentic Skills at Scale
blurb: This paper introduces an evaluation framework that lets skill authors build realistic tasks to measure how much a given agent skill actually improves an LLM agent. The authors applied it at scale to 500 real-world skills, generating 1,000 tasks across 19 proprietary and open-source model configurations, and found that models vary widely in how closely they follow skill instructions.

title: SoK: AI-Augmented Binary Reversing
blurb: This paper presents the first systematization of knowledge on AI-augmented binary reversing, analyzing 144 papers published since 2015 across 22 reversing domains organized by inference task. The authors introduce a unified taxonomy connecting traditional analysis techniques, binary-derived artifacts, representation strategies, and learning paradigms, while clarifying the emerging roles of LLMs and agentic systems and flagging persistent evaluation gaps.

title: Nvidia plans to raise at least $20 billion in its first debt sale since start of AI boom
blurb: Nvidia plans to raise at least $20 billion in its first bond sale since the AI boom began, with sources saying the total could approach $25 billion. The chipmaker said proceeds would go toward general corporate purposes and refinancing existing debt, joining Alphabet, Amazon, and Super Micro in tapping capital markets despite generating $49 billion in free cash flow last quarter.

title: SpaceX’s planned $60 billion deal for Cursor raises questions for CIOs
blurb: SpaceX officially announced plans to acquire AI coding startup Cursor for $60 billion in stock, formalizing terms it previewed in April, including a $10 billion breakup fee. Analysts say the deal would give Cursor, used by 64% of the Fortune 500 and earning roughly $2 billion annually, access to xAI's GPU capacity that had bottlenecked its growth.

title: Google develops Personalization controls for Gemini
blurb: Google is building personalization controls for Gemini that prioritize paid subscriptions and let users inspect and edit gathered context on a per-app basis.

title: AI Agent Skill Managers Are a Supply-Chain Surface Most of Them Don't Guard
blurb: Three open-source managers for AI agent skills, the SKILL.md packages that tools like Claude Code and Codex read and run, each passed 2,000 GitHub stars within months. Because a skill is natural-language intent executed with full file and shell access, every manager becomes a software supply-chain channel. Only one of the three scans skill files for malicious instructions before an agent acts on them.

title: Qualcomm wants to be the chip inside whatever replaces your smartphone, and it just announced two products toward that end
blurb: Qualcomm announced Snapdragon Reality Elite, a mixed-reality glasses platform, and START, a turnkey toolkit of hardware modules and software for building AI smart glasses. Reality Elite delivers up to 160% better NPU performance than its predecessor and can run a 3-billion-parameter language model at 45 tokens per second, with XREAL Project Aura among the first devices to use it.

title: General Atlantic in Talks to Lead Round for China’s Kling AI
blurb: Kuaishou is in talks with General Atlantic to lead a first financing round for its Kling AI video unit, seeking $2B at an $18B valuation. The deal would secure a major US backer ahead of a planned IPO for the video generation business.

title: Databricks’ new agentic coworker Genie One brings AI automation to every part of the business
blurb: Databricks released Genie One, an agentic coworker that orchestrates and automates workflows for sales, marketing, and finance teams by reasoning over structured and unstructured data inside and outside its platform. The tool runs on Genie Ontology, a self-improving context layer that maps an organization's knowledge by scanning its data, documents, applications, and employees to ground actions in real business context.

title: Sarvam Becomes India's Latest AI Unicorn
blurb: Sarvam AI raised $234M from investors including HCL Tech, Bessemer, and Khosla Ventures, making it India's latest AI unicorn. The round positions the startup among India's most heavily funded efforts to build sovereign AI models tailored to the country's languages and markets.

title: Learning Cardiac Electrophysiology Digital Twins Through Agentic Discovery of Hybrid Structure
blurb: LEADS is a framework that formulates cardiac electrophysiology domain knowledge as a structured action space and uses an LLM agent to discover patient-specific hybrid physics-neural models. The agent runs an iterative reasoning-and-action loop to select, combine, and refine architectures while gradient descent fits parameters, outperforming both human-designed hybrid models and prior LLM-based methods on synthetic and real cardiac data.

title: When LLMs Analyze Scars: From Images to Clinically-Meaningful Features
blurb: This paper introduces ScaFE, a framework that repositions LLMs as feature engineers rather than classifiers by prompting them to generate deterministic Python code that extracts clinically interpretable scar features aligned with scoring systems like the Vancouver Scar Scale. The approach processes images locally for privacy and outperforms end-to-end deep learning and black-box LLM classifiers on keloid versus hypertrophic scar classification under limited training data.

title: PseudoBench: Measuring How Agentic Auto-Research Fuels Pseudoscience
blurb: PseudoBench is an adversarial benchmark that tests whether agentic auto-research systems can identify and resist pseudoscientific claims across 200 claim-evidence pairs in five domains. Testing seven state-of-the-art agents, the authors found near-zero refusal rates and a peak resistance of just 27.4%, with stronger agents packaging pseudoscience in more sophisticated scientific language that increased its apparent credibility.

title: Xreal unveils Aura, its lightweight smart glasses powered by Android XR
blurb: Xreal released Aura, sub-95-gram wired smart glasses running Android XR on Qualcomm's Snapdragon, with a 70-degree field of view, shipping this fall.

title: Learning task-specific subspaces via interventional post-training of speech foundation models
blurb: This paper proposes a post-training method that uses interventional contrastive learning to split the entangled representations of speech foundation models into separate content and speaker subspaces. Evaluated on speaker verification and keyword spotting, the approach improves out-of-domain speaker verification and shows that speaker and content information become disentangled across the learned subspaces.

title: Safety, Security, and Cognitive Risks in Neuro-Symbolic AI
blurb: This paper maps the security risks of neuro-symbolic AI, which pairs neural perception with symbolic reasoning across five exploitable layers: perception, knowledge bases, reasoning engines, agentic orchestration, and data stores. The authors formalize a NeSy attack surface and threat model extending MITRE ATLAS, then show empirically that targeted knowledge graph poisoning, adversarial perturbations, and single-axiom OWL edits achieve up to 93.3% symbolic integrity violation while evading consistency checks.

title: Adaptive Volumetric Mechanical Property Fields Invariant to Resolution
blurb: AdaVoMP predicts dense spatially-varying material properties for 3D objects at 16³ higher resolution than prior art, producing simulation-ready assets.

title: ChLogic: Evaluating Robustness of Logical Reasoning in Chinese Expressions
blurb: ChLogic is an English-Chinese aligned benchmark that tests whether large language models preserve logical reasoning when the same latent logical structure is expressed across five Chinese surface realizations versus an English reference. Experiments on Qwen3, Ministral, and GLM reveal a persistent English-Chinese performance gap, and back-translation into English helps on general problems but degrades Qwen3-32B and GLM-5.1 on harder ones.

title: Toward Accessible Psychotherapy Training Using AI-Driven Interactive Patient Avatars
blurb: This paper introduces a system for training psychotherapists in Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT) through spoken dialogue with an embodied virtual patient, using LLMs to simulate patient behavior from real-session profiles while a separate evaluator scores therapist responses against ACT fidelity criteria. Across 49 therapy transcripts, GPT-4o-mini produced the best feedback, matching human supervisor fidelity ratings with the lowest mean absolute error of 6.12.

title: AI’s constant patching treadmill can be a security problem
blurb: Backslash Security found that Anthropic quietly shipped more than 30 security-relevant fixes to Claude Code between April and early June 2026, covering data poisoning, prompt injection, and arbitrary code execution flaws. One patch closed a bypass that let a single backslash trigger catastrophic deletion commands, while others addressed leaked OAuth credentials and an agent planting a backdoor in shell startup files.

title: Knowledge Reutilization in Meta-Reinforcement Learning
blurb: This paper introduces a meta-reinforcement learning framework that learns task-level knowledge on a dynamics-simplified agent and transfers it to heterogeneous agents through a semantic-magnitude interface and a lightweight temporal adaptor. Across multiple locomotion agents, the method cuts final-step tracking error by 94.75% to 99.79% versus recent baselines while reaching comparable deployment performance using about 23.8% of their interaction data.

title: Position: Coding Benchmarks Are Misaligned with Agentic Software Engineering
blurb: This paper argues that current coding benchmarks are misaligned with how agents actually do software engineering. It contends that a coding agent is a system, not a model, combining harnesses, contexts, environments, and feedback signals that can each shift scores as much as a model generation. The authors flag three symptoms: scores conflate the model with the harness, single-reference grading penalizes valid alternatives, and component-level signal for iteration is missing.

title: A Diffusion Approximation for Temporal-Difference Learning with Linear Features under Markovian Noise
blurb: This paper introduces a stochastic differential equation approximation for linear TD(0) policy evaluation under Markovian noise, refining the classical ODE model that captures only asymptotic mean dynamics and ignores the fluctuations setting the error floor. The SDE separates the contraction dynamics of the projected Bellman operator from Markovian sampling, explaining the constant-stepsize error floor through the interaction between long-run covariance and contraction geometry.

title: Fine-tuning LLMs for Passive Depression Severity Estimation from AI Mental Health Dialogue
blurb: This paper fine-tunes a Qwen3.5-27B model with a regression head to predict PHQ-9 depression scores directly from user conversations with an AI mental health app, requiring only transcript text and no clinical data. Trained on 6,283 users with Claude Opus pseudolabels, the best model reaches MAE 2.6, Pearson r = 0.80, and AUC 0.91 at the PHQ-9 ≥ 10 clinical threshold on a held-out set of 842 users.

title: When English Isn't the Best Teacher: Source Language Effects in Cross-Lingual In-Context Learning
blurb: This paper tests whether cross-lingual transfer heuristics from supervised fine-tuning still hold for few-shot in-context learning, running an empirical study across seven tasks, six models, and a typologically diverse set of languages. The authors find that conventional fine-tuning expectations do not consistently apply in the ICL regime, analyze language confusion in generative tasks, and propose alternative heuristics for selecting source languages.

title: Databricks pitches LTAP as a new foundation for agentic applications
blurb: Databricks introduced LTAP, an architecture unifying transactional and analytical data on one lakehouse layer to give AI agents live and historical context without ETL pipelines.

title: Sources: Meta decided to support KOSA after it was packaged with language preempting state AI laws and a bill requiring app stores to verify users' ages (Politico)
blurb: Meta decided to support the Kids Online Safety Act (KOSA) after it was bundled with language preempting state AI laws and a provision requiring app stores to verify users' ages. The packaging ties child-safety legislation to a federal preemption of the growing patchwork of state-level AI rules, a long-standing priority for large tech platforms.

title: Planning to Hammer: Difficulty-Aware Decomposition for Automating Rocq Proofs
blurb: Quarry is a planning-based proof synthesis framework that separates proof planning from execution by having an LLM propose multiple sublemma decompositions, type-checking them in Rocq, and ranking candidates with a difficulty model that estimates how solvable each goal is by an automated hammer like CoqHammer. Under a uniform 10-minute budget, it improves success rate by 7% to 13% over the strongest baseline across three Rocq benchmarks.

title: PromptMN: Pseudo Prompting Language
blurb: This paper introduces PromptMN, a pseudo-prompting domain-specific language that annotates natural language with compact, %-prefixed typed directives for roles, goals, constraints, plans, inputs, and outputs. Tested across Claude Opus 4.8, Gemini 3.1 Pro, and GPT-5.5 without fine-tuning, the models correctly resolved structures like conditionals and a prime-checking task, and pairing it with reverse prompt engineering lets users inspect inferred assumptions before acting.

title: Collaborative Large and Small Language Models for Accurate and Scalable Data Repair
blurb: LasRepair is a data-repair framework that pairs an LLM instructor, which selects a global repair context, with a small language model corrector that uses that context to fix erroneous entries more efficiently. The authors extend it with an Expectation-Maximisation loop and column-calibrated confidence weighting to down-weight unreliable rows, reporting an average 18.1% F1-score improvement over the strongest baseline.

title: A comparison of human and LLM-simulated participants in a writing style task
blurb: This study tests whether an LLM prompted as a synthetic participant can replace humans, comparing GPT-4o simulations against 30 human participants in a writing style preference task. The authors find bias and a lack of depth in GPT-4o's text generation and judgment that prevent it from accurately reproducing human behavior, while also surfacing human biases that matter for evaluating human-automation systems. They frame the discrepancies not as a verdict on LLM-simulated participants but as a case analysis of the methodological and design challenges involved.

title: Sources: Samsung is seeing a rise in advanced chip production requests from BYD, Google, AMD, and others as TSMC's capacity remains constrained by AI demand (Nikkei Asia)
blurb: Samsung is seeing rising demand for advanced chip production from BYD, Google, AMD, and other customers as TSMC's capacity stays constrained by AI workloads, according to Nikkei Asia. The shift signals that TSMC's dominance in leading-edge manufacturing is leaving enough unmet demand to push major chip designers toward Samsung's foundry as a secondary source.

title: “세일즈포스가 산 것은 AI가 아니라 수익모델” 핀 인수의 진짜 의미
blurb: Salesforce announced plans to acquire agentic AI company Fin, formerly known as Intercom, to strengthen its customer-service agent strategy and expand into the SMB market. Fin handles end-to-end customer inquiries across live chat, email, WhatsApp, SMS, phone, and Slack, and brings roughly 30,000 mostly small-business customers, with the deal expected to close in Salesforce's fiscal Q4 2027.

title: Memory as a Wasting Asset: Pricing Flash Endurance for Embodied Agents, and the Limits of Doing So
blurb: This paper models a robot's flash memory endurance as depreciating capital, pricing each program/erase cycle with a single endurance shadow price that turns cost-optimal placement across RAM, on-board NVM, and cloud into a wear-augmented threshold rule. Measured on real robot logs, the value-write association is positive on recurrent long-horizon manipulation and negative on teleoperation, but the budget only binds on commodity QLC/eMMC hardware rather than premium TLC.

title: Evaluating Open-Source LLMs for Multi-Label ATT&CK Technique Classification on CTI Reports
blurb: This paper benchmarks seven open-source LLMs ranging from 8B to 236B parameters on multi-label MITRE ATT&CK technique classification, using a new ground-truth dataset of 2,076 human-annotated sentences drawn from 83 complex CTI reports. The best model reached a micro-averaged F1 of just 0.22, and while larger parameter counts correlated with better scores, prompt strategy and temperature produced no significant gains, indicating current open-source models are insufficient for production-grade classification.

title: China pushes for AI safety as G7 summit wraps up without Beijing
blurb: Chinese officials announced plans to accelerate a global AI cooperation organization and released a governance whitepaper emphasizing cheap, openly downloadable models for the Global South. The push came as G7 countries, meeting in France without Beijing, discussed giving "trusted partners" access to subscription-based US AI models, underscoring diverging approaches to distributing and restricting the technology.

title: Mind Companion: An Embodied Conversational Agent for Process-Based Psychotherapy
blurb: Mind Companion is an LLM-based embodied conversational agent for process-based psychotherapy that runs real-time analysis of client statements across fact extraction, psychological flexibility detection, emotion recognition, and safety monitoring for supervising clinicians. In evaluations against real therapist responses by automated LLM judges and 11 professional psychotherapists, GPT-5.2 scored higher than human therapists on understanding, interpersonal effectiveness, collaboration, and therapeutic alignment.

title: Anthropic’s latest feud with the Trump admin may actually help it, sales data suggests
blurb: Anthropic surpassed OpenAI in business AI spending market share for the first time in May, per Ramp data, days before the Trump administration ordered it to bar non-Americans from its top Mythos and Fable 5 models, forcing both off the market. Ramp's lead economist argues the feud will likely boost adoption, noting Anthropic's best month on record came when the Pentagon labeled it a supply-chain risk.

title: SketchXplain: Intuitive Visual Explanations of Image Classifiers with Sketches
blurb: SketchXplain generates sketch-based visual explanations for image classifiers, combining saliency maps, concept-bottleneck models, and sketch optimization to address the semantic gap left by conventional saliency visualizations. In user studies on face expression recognition and skin lesion diagnosis, it supported quicker interpretation and more coherent symptom visualization than saliency maps or simple drawings.

title: MemSlides: A Hierarchical Memory Driven Agent Framework for Personalized Slide Generation with Multi-turn Local Revision
blurb: MemSlides is a hierarchical memory framework for personalized presentation agents that separates long-term memory, split into user profile and tool memory, from working memory that carries active preferences across revision rounds. It pairs this design with scoped slide-local revision, editing only the smallest affected region instead of regenerating the full deck, and controlled experiments show gains in persona alignment and closed-loop modification behavior.

title: A profile of Anthropic researcher Nicholas Carlini, who warned about Mythos in March and is now part of an Anthropic team briefing the White House on safeguards (Wall Street Journal)
blurb: The Wall Street Journal profiled Anthropic researcher Nicholas Carlini, who flagged risks from Mythos in March and now sits on an Anthropic team briefing the White House on AI safeguards. The piece traces how an internal safety warning escalated into direct engagement with federal policymakers on guardrails for frontier systems.

title: Dealogic: investors have poured $58B across 42 data center deals YTD; Oxford Economics says ~850 data centers worth some $7T are under construction globally (Financial Times)
blurb: Investors have committed $58B across 42 data center deals so far this year, according to Dealogic, as the sector's buildout accelerates. Oxford Economics estimates roughly 850 data centers worth about $7T are now under construction globally, underscoring the scale of capital flowing into AI compute infrastructure.

title: Lawmakers leary about Trump administration’s Anthropic order
blurb: Members of Congress responded with skepticism to the Trump administration's order imposing export controls on Anthropic's newest models, which the company said forced it to disable Fable 5 and Mythos 5. Several Democrats, including Sen. Angus King, questioned whether the cited national security concerns were legitimate given the administration's prior disputes with Anthropic over domestic surveillance and autonomous weapons use.

title: Your AI Travel Agent Would Book You a Bullfight: An Agentic Benchmark for Implicit Animal Welfare in Frontier AI Models
blurb: This paper introduces TAC (Travel Agent Compassion), the first agentic benchmark measuring whether AI agents avoid animal-exploitation options when booking travel on a user's behalf across 48 scenarios. All seven frontier models tested scored below the 64% chance level, with Claude Opus 4.7 leading at 53%, though a single welfare-aware sentence in the system prompt lifted Claude and GPT-5.5 by 47 to 63 percentage points.

title: xAI is working on Automations feature for Grok
blurb: xAI is building an Automations feature for Grok that folds in scheduling, Skills selection, and model choice, replacing the standalone Tasks tool.

title: OmniPlan: An Adaptive Framework for Timely and Near-Optimal Network Planning Optimization
blurb: OmniPlan is an adaptive framework for network planning optimization that uses an LLM to convert natural-language user intents into a quantifiable preference vector. It routes those intents through a mixture-of-experts architecture combining MIP solvers, heuristics, and DRL models, and in real-world ML inference offloading tests it cut latency by up to 97.8% and network device resource consumption by up to 11.5%.

title: INI-VPINN: A Variational Physics-Informed Neural Network with Implicit Neumann and Interface Handling for Multi-Material Domains with Geometric Singularities
blurb: INI-VPINN is a weak-form physics-informed neural network that bakes Neumann and interface conditions into its variational formulation, improving accuracy on multi-material domains.

title: Trump admin tries to block Clean Air Act lawsuit over xAI's gas turbines
blurb: The Trump administration's Justice Department urged a federal judge to dismiss an NAACP Clean Air Act lawsuit against xAI, arguing the suit threatens national security because Grok supports military operations. The NAACP alleges xAI ran up to 57 unpermitted gas turbines at its Colossus gas plant in Southaven, Mississippi, which powers the Colossus 2 data center behind Grok.

title: MAJIC: Leveraging Articulatory Motion for Speech-based Emotion Recognition
blurb: MAJIC is a multimodal emotion recognition system that pairs audio features with articulatory motion, including jaw movements and facial muscle vibrations, to detect emotion in speech where audio-only systems struggle with subtle expressions. Evaluated on data from 20 participants across 10 languages and both prompted and conversational settings, it reached 93% accuracy and a 91% F1 score, outperforming strong audio-based baselines.

title: The Hacker Sent by Anthropic to Calm the Government’s Nerves About AI Safety
blurb: Anthropic recruited Nicholas Carlini, a researcher who previously warned publicly about AI's dangers, to help make the case to government officials that its latest models are safe to release. His shift from raising alarms to defending deployment illustrates the tension inside frontier labs between safety advocacy and the commercial pressure to ship.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Minimum Zero-Forcing Sets
blurb: This paper proposes SD-ZFS, a reinforcement learning framework that adapts the S2V-DQN architecture to find minimum zero-forcing sets on undirected graphs, an NP-hard graph coloring problem with applications in network control and logic circuit design. The authors train models across graph datasets with varying structures and evaluate generalization, scaling, and transfer, comparing results against optimal solutions and a greedy heuristic.

title: When deep research isn't enough for your business: Sakana AI launches 'ultra deep research' agent for 100+ page reports in 8 hours
blurb: Sakana AI launched Marlin, a "Virtual CSO" agent that runs eight-hour reasoning loops to produce 100-page strategy reports.

title: Sign-Rank, Index, and List Replicability: Connections and Separations
blurb: This paper proves a strong separation between sign rank and the Z₂-index, and bounds list replicability by combinatorial measures like height and star number.

title: Reading between the Lines: Leveraging Large Language Models for Global Dementia and Depression Assessment from Clinical Interviews
blurb: This study tests open-weights LLMs (Mistral 3.1, DeepHermes, Qwen3) for predicting dementia and depression severity from speech in standardized clinical interviews with 154 German-speaking subjects. LLMs predicted depression well zero-shot (best MAE 0.60), while dementia assessment improved with structured feature extraction feeding Support Vector Regression (best MAE 0.78, cutting errors up to 35%), and pause-enriched automatic transcripts matched human transcriptions.

title: Unlocking LLM Code Correction with Iterative Feedback Loops
blurb: This study evaluates whether LLMs can fix their own code using execution feedback, testing four models across two programming languages with compiler errors and test-case results fed back after each attempt. Reasoning models consistently improved over iterations and substantially outperformed non-reasoning models at leveraging feedback, while syntactic and runtime errors proved far more tractable than logical or algorithmic failures.

title: OmniDroneX: An LLM-Assisted Holistic Drone-as-a-Service Ecosystem
blurb: OmniDroneX is a proposed Drone-as-a-Service ecosystem that turns fixed-function UAVs into dynamically composable entities through a vendor-agnostic interface (libUAV) and a formal physical-service abstraction model (PT-SOA). LLMs operate across multiple layers to formalize device functions, automate service composition and workflow generation, and let operators specify and refine missions in natural language.

title: Trust-Aware Multi-Agent Traceability: Confidence-Calibrated Knowledge Graphs for Consistent Software Artifact Management
blurb: This paper proposes a trust-aware coordination framework for multi-agent software engineering pipelines, using a shared knowledge graph as semantic memory where agents build on each other's contributions through calibrated confidence scores. The approach combines embedding-based retrieval with LLM multi-criteria analysis for traceability link prediction, plus a consistency protocol with confidence threshold gating and conflict resolution, evaluated on an automotive case study where ablations confirm calibration is essential.

title: HistoRAG: Embedding Historical Methodology in Retrieval-Augmented Generation Through Critical Technical Practice
blurb: HistoRAG is a framework that translates historiographical principles into concrete RAG architecture changes, decoupling source retrieval from interpretation, enforcing temporal windowing for balanced source representation, and adding LLM-as-judge relevance evaluation. Tested on 102,189 Der Spiegel articles from 1950-1979, the authors show standard RAG retrieves zero 1950s chunks when queried with 1970s vocabulary, and that vector similarity correlates weakly with LLM-assessed relevance (Spearman rho = 0.275).

title: When AI Says "I have been in similar situations": Synthetic Lived Experience in Peer-Like Caregiver Support
blurb: This paper examines a "synthetic lived experience paradox" in AI caregiver support: LLMs prompted to sound peer-like can generate experiential language that falsely positions them as having lived through caregiving. Analyzing ADRD caregiver exchanges against responses from LLaMA, GPT-4o-mini, and MedGemma, the authors find human peers use significantly more first-person and past-focused language, while AI captures the emotional work but fabricates experiential grounding. They argue caregiver-support systems need mechanisms to separate supportive framing from invented lived experience.

title: Embedded Machine Learning for Microcontroller-Class Edge Devices: Data, Feature, Evaluation, and Deployment Pipelines
blurb: This paper presents a systems-oriented synthesis of the embedded machine learning workflow for microcontroller-class devices, covering sampling and buffering, feature extraction, validation under class imbalance, model/runtime co-design, and streaming deployment. It works through two examples, inertial motion recognition using accelerometer spectral features and keyword spotting using MFCCs and a compact 1D convolutional network, then closes with design rules for quantization, thresholding, scheduling, and field monitoring.

title: A Convex Quasilinearization Method for Solving Nonlinear PDEs with Physics-Informed Neural Networks
blurb: This paper introduces LiL-Q, a method for solving nonlinear PDEs that uses Bellman-Kalaba quasilinearization to reduce the problem to a sequence of linear subproblems, each solved by a single direct least-squares QR factorization instead of nonconvex gradient-based PINN training. Across seven benchmarks including Burgers, Darcy flow, and 2D/3D Navier-Stokes, it converges in single-digit iterations and matches or beats published PINN solvers with up to two orders of magnitude fewer trainable parameters.

title: LLM-as-Judge in Education: A Curriculum-Grounded Marking Pipeline
blurb: This paper presents a curriculum-grounded LLM-as-Judge pipeline for question-level exam marking, co-developed with an industrial partner to support university admission prep. The system identifies a question's topics and cognitive demand, then grounds judgments in authorised syllabus artefacts like prescribed verbs, performance band descriptors, and marking-guideline principles, producing marks comparable to human tutors with more traceable justifications.

title: Bland raises $50M to automate complex, high-stakes phone calls
blurb: Bland raised $50M in a Series C led by Dell Technologies Capital to scale its voice AI agents into more regulated industries. Unlike rivals that wrap third-party models around scripted tasks, Bland runs voice models it built in-house to handle long, nonlinear calls, and it now processes more than 3.5 million calls a week across healthcare, financial services, and other regulated sectors.

title: SpaceX to acquire AI coding platform Cursor for $60 billion
blurb: SpaceX agreed to acquire AI coding platform Cursor for $60 billion in all-stock, two days after its IPO and months after absorbing xAI. The deal rescues a company that was reportedly struggling to break even as Anthropic's Claude Code took the dominant position Cursor once held. The logic is compute: Cursor said its growth was bottlenecked on infrastructure, xAI already wired it into its data centers, and the two had started co-training models like Grok Build. Owning the IDE gives SpaceX a distribution surface for those models against Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google.

title: SpaceX is public: Everything you need to know post-IPO
blurb: SpaceX completed the largest IPO in history, pricing 555.6 million shares at $135 to raise $85.7 billion and making Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire. Shares opened at $150, climbed past $160, and pushed the valuation to $2.7T, fifth-most valuable company in the world and ahead of Amazon. Days later SpaceX announced a $60B all-stock deal to acquire Cursor, while COO Gwynne Shotwell floated a SpaceX-Tesla merger and banks led by Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley collected roughly $500M in fees.

title: Elon Musk Is Unleashing SpaceX’s New War Chest to Solve His AI Problem
blurb: Musk is routing SpaceX's cash into his AI ambitions, reportedly buying coding agent Cursor in a $60B deal and renting out data-center capacity to court enterprise customers.
