blurb: HYPE, UNI, and WLD each posted double-digit gains as bitcoin and the broader market drifted lower, with HYPE hitting a record above $76 and now up nearly 200% in 2026. Uniswap rallied 18% after Standard Chartered initiated bullish coverage on DeFi's growth potential, while Worldcoin extended its AI-linked run and Hyperliquid drew flows on its onchain perpetuals exchange and deepening USDC arrangement.

title: Coinbase to join tokenized stock race with onchain shares, dividend payments
blurb: Coinbase said it will launch tokenized stocks backed one-for-one by underlying U.S. equities, giving holders direct onchain ownership with automatic dividend payments rather than the derivative or synthetic exposure most existing offerings provide. The products will debut in eligible jurisdictions outside the U.S. on an unannounced date, positioning Coinbase against the broader Wall Street and crypto push to move traditional securities onto blockchain rails.

title: Coinbase launching tokenized US stocks, backed 1:1, with holders able to receive dividends
blurb: Coinbase is launching tokenized US stocks backed 1:1 by underlying shares, with token holders able to receive dividends directly on-chain. The move pushes Coinbase into a contested tokenized-equities market where Robinhood, Kraken, and traditional financial institutions are all building competing offerings, blurring the line between brokerage and crypto exchange.

title: Ripple buys into Flutterwave Series E at $3.2 billion valuation, backs RLUSD integration
blurb: Ripple invested in Flutterwave's Series E at a $3.2B valuation, embedding its RLUSD stablecoin and the XRP Ledger into the African payments company's infrastructure across 34 markets. The partnership targets Flutterwave's cross-border corridors and Send App remittances, using RLUSD as a settlement asset and the XRP Ledger for faster clearing to cut multi-day delays and high FX margins.

title: State Street launches GENIUS-compliant money market fund for stablecoin issuers
blurb: State Street launched SSCXX, a Rule 2a-7 government money market fund built to comply with the GENIUS Act and serve as a reserve vehicle for stablecoin issuers. The fund invests only in cash and government cash equivalents, giving issuers a regulated, yield-bearing home for reserves as the GENIUS framework formalizes backing requirements and pulls established asset managers into stablecoin infrastructure.

title: Ethereum’s biggest protocol overhaul in years moves into its final development stage
blurb: Ethereum developers entered the final development phase of Glamsterdam, running internal devnets with the full slate of planned EIPs before the upgrade moves to public testnets, with activation expected in the second half of the year. The fork, described as the network's largest since the 2022 Merge, enshrines proposer-builder separation (EIP-7732) and block-level access lists (EIP-7928) to curb MEV manipulation and set up further scaling.

title: Trump-backed World Liberty Financial nears OCC approval for federal trust charter: report
blurb: World Liberty Financial, the crypto venture backed by President Trump's family, is nearing OCC approval for a federal trust bank charter, according to a report. The charter would let World Liberty issue and redeem its USD1 stablecoin under a single federal regulator, removing the need to navigate state-by-state money-transmitter licensing.

title: State Street targets stablecoin reserve boom with new money market fund
blurb: State Street launched a government money market fund built specifically for stablecoin issuers, joining BlackRock, Franklin Templeton, Fidelity and JPMorgan in the race to manage the Treasury bills and cash backing digital dollars under the GENIUS Act. With global stablecoin issuance projected to reach $4 trillion by 2030, asset managers are treating reserve management as a fast-growing pool of fee-generating assets.

title: Binance may be forced to halt services for EU clients next month: Reuters
blurb: Binance may have to suspend services for EU clients next month, Reuters reported, as the exchange struggles to secure MiCA authorization before the bloc's July 1 deadline to register or cease operating. A forced halt would strand European users and hand market share to MiCA-licensed rivals like Coinbase and OKX, underscoring how binding the regime has become for crypto firms operating in the EU.

title: BlackRock's new bitcoin income fund offers cash flow alongside BTC exposure
blurb: BlackRock launched the iShares Bitcoin Premium Income ETF (BITA), which holds spot bitcoin and IBIT shares while selling call options on 25% to 35% of the portfolio to generate monthly income. The covered-call structure targets income-focused investors and bitcoin holders seeking cash flow, arriving after IBIT's nearly $49B haul and with bitcoin down about 23% year to date around $67,000.

title: BlackRock's new bitcoin ETF lets institutions earn from volatility. There's a catch.
blurb: BlackRock is expected to debut the iShares Bitcoin Premium Income ETF (BITA), which holds its IBIT spot ETF and sells call options against it to target a 15% annual yield from bitcoin's volatility. The catch is that systematic call overwriting at BlackRock's scale suppresses bitcoin's implied volatility and caps investor upside, retaining only about 70% of the underlying's appreciation in exchange for steadier income.

title: A look at Wyoming's Frontier Stable Token, which launched in January and currently has a market value of ~$1M, as other states explore "white labeling" the tech (David Voreacos/Bloomberg)
blurb: Wyoming's Frontier Stable Token (FRNT), the first stablecoin issued by a US state, launched in January and now carries a market value of roughly $1M. Other states are exploring "white labeling" Wyoming's underlying tech to issue their own tokens, suggesting state-level public stablecoins could proliferate even as federal frameworks like the GENIUS Act take shape.

title: Robinhood cuts 10% of staff, expects $28 million in restructuring charges
blurb: Robinhood cut 10% of its staff and expects roughly $28 million in restructuring charges, tied to flattening management layers and a broader efficiency push. The reductions signal that even profitable retail brokerages are trimming headcount as growth normalizes, prioritizing leaner org structures over the aggressive hiring that defined the meme-stock and crypto-trading boom.

title: World Cup Exposes Growing Rift Over Prediction Markets
blurb: Prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket are letting users bet on World Cup outcomes at scale for the first time, exposing a regulatory divide between jurisdictions that treat the contracts as sports gambling and those that allow them as event derivatives. The split leaves operators navigating conflicting rules on licensing and consumer protection, with the CFTC's hands-off stance in the US clashing with stricter gaming regulators abroad.

title: An AI Security Agent for Banking: Multi-Vector Fraud and AML Detection Across Retail and Corporate Accounts
blurb: This paper presents an AI security agent for retail and corporate banking that runs parallel transaction and session streams, each fusing an LSTM behavioral model, a velocity monitor, and a graph module tracking fan-in/fan-out and pass-through ratios for AML detection. On a synthetic log of 237,669 transactions across 13 threat categories, it hit F1 of 0.787 and 0.867, beating rule-based (0.562/0.733) and LSTM-only baselines, with Critical-tier response under 0.43 ms.

title: SpaceX Crypto Short Squeeze Briefly Sends Value to $3T
blurb: A short squeeze in SpaceX perpetual futures briefly pushed the company's implied valuation to $3T on blockchain-based prediction markets shortly after Monday's US stock-market close. The episode shows how tokenized markets for private companies can manufacture wild price swings on thin liquidity, raising questions about how regulators will treat on-chain valuations of firms that never filed to trade publicly.

title: U.S. senators urge Treasury not to leave states out of GENIUS Act stablecoin process
blurb: A bipartisan group of senators led by Cynthia Lummis pressed Treasury to give states a clear certification process for proving their stablecoin oversight is on par with federal supervision under the GENIUS Act. The senators warned that Treasury's proposed principles omit timelines and procedural requirements, leaving state regulators uncertain about next steps and risking a process that effectively forecloses state-level participation in stablecoin supervision.

title: Binance says its European regulatory application is compliant despite report of Greek rejection
blurb: Binance said its Greek MiCA license application was deemed compliant by the Hellenic Capital Market Commission, pushing back on a Reuters report that the regulator is set to reject it before the EU's end-of-month deadline. A rejection would block the world's largest crypto exchange from serving EU customers under MiCA, forcing it to reapply through another member state's regulator.

title: HSBC Expects Over $100 Million Gains From Using Google AI Tools
blurb: HSBC will deploy Google Cloud AI across its global operations, with individual projects each expected to generate over $100 million in added revenue or cost savings. The bank's commitment signals how large incumbents are betting on hyperscaler AI partnerships to wring efficiency from sprawling operations, intensifying competition among cloud providers for financial-services workloads.

title: China Watchdog Urges More IPOs From AI, Hong Kong Listed Firms
blurb: China's securities regulator, the CSRC, pledged to welcome more domestic listings from AI developers and Hong Kong-traded firms to deepen onshore capital markets. The push aims to keep high-growth tech companies listing at home rather than overseas, signaling Beijing wants its strategic AI sector funded through mainland exchanges as US listings stay constrained.

title: How Kalshi’s Lopes Lara Turned Kylie Jenner Gossip Into Billions
blurb: Kalshi built its own AI agent to handle internal operations, including the contentious work of drafting and refining the language of its prediction-market contracts. The move shows how prediction-market platforms are leaning on automation to manage contract-design risk as trading volumes scale into the billions and regulatory scrutiny of event-contract wording intensifies.

title: Beijing’s New Message to Its Citizens: Your Money Belongs at Home
blurb: China is tightening restrictions on how individual investors can access global markets, limiting outbound vehicles like the Qualified Domestic Institutional Investor program and cross-border products to keep capital onshore. The clampdown raises friction for brokerages, wealth platforms, and payment intermediaries that route Chinese retail money abroad, signaling Beijing will prioritize defending the yuan and domestic liquidity over financial openness.

title: Paradigm Leads $9 Million Round in Latin American Stablecoin App El Dorado
blurb: Paradigm led a roughly $9M round in El Dorado, a stablecoin payments SuperApp that connects USDT to local rails across Argentina, Brazil, Colombia, Peru and other Latin American markets. The app, which has crossed one million users and settles via USDT on Arbitrum to cut costs, deepens Paradigm's bet on dollar rails in emerging markets where capital controls and inflation limit access to US dollars.

title: Here is why Strategy's dividend-paying crypto stock is crashing to near-historic lows
blurb: Strategy's bitcoin-backed preferred stock STRC closed at $91.79 on Tuesday, its third-lowest level since launching in July 2025 and nearly 8% below its $100 par value, as falling bitcoin prices and thinning dividend coverage weigh on the security. After repaying $1.5B in convertible debt, Strategy has roughly seven months of dividend runway left, pushing yield-seekers toward Strive's debt-free SATA, which trades near par at a 13% annualized yield.

title: Bipartisan senators push Treasury to uphold states’ authorities under the GENIUS Act
blurb: A bipartisan group of senators urged the Treasury Department to preserve states' authority to regulate stablecoin issuers under the GENIUS Act, pushing back on a federal interpretation that could centralize oversight. The letter signals that state regulators may retain a meaningful role in licensing and supervising stablecoin issuers, shaping the compliance path for fintechs weighing state versus federal charters.

title: Crypto PAC's $12 million Senate candidate, Barry Moore, wins Alabama GOP primary
blurb: Crypto super PAC Fairshake spent more than $12 million backing Rep. Barry Moore, who won Alabama's Republican Senate runoff with about 56% of the vote, its largest single outlay this cycle. With roughly $150 million still on hand, Fairshake is positioned to keep building a pro-crypto Senate caucus, signaling the industry's growing leverage over the regulatory agenda heading into November.

title: BitGo offers Europe’s crypto firms a MiCA-compliance lifeline as license deadline looms
blurb: BitGo Europe, authorized by BaFin, opened its Crypto-as-a-Service platform to crypto firms scrambling to meet MiCA's end-of-month transition deadline, letting them onboard clients into MiCA-compliant segregated sub-accounts instead of building their own regulated stack. Hogan Lovells estimates roughly 75% of Europe's pre-MiCA crypto firms will lose registration status as transitional periods expire, leaving only 194 authorized CASPs against more than 3,000 prior registrants.

title: SpaceX Investor Powerlaw Debuts Amid IPO Race
blurb: Powerlaw, a closed-end fund holding stakes in SpaceX and OpenAI, debuted at a price well above its underlying net asset value as retail investors chased exposure to the hottest private tech names. The premium signals demand for liquid wrappers around illiquid private equity, a structure that lets ordinary investors buy in ahead of any IPO but at a markup to the assets they actually own.

title: Top Philippine Fintech GCash Owner Wins Approval for Jumbo IPO
blurb: Mynt, the Ant Group- and Globe Telecom-backed parent of GCash, secured board and shareholder approval for what could be the Philippines' largest-ever IPO. The listing would crown GCash's run from QR-payments app to dominant national wallet, and signals that Southeast Asian fintech is reaching the public-market scale that has so far eluded US neobanks.
